title: Nephrodium rigidum
taxonomy: Nephrodium rigidum is classified as a species of fern, more specifically within the family Dryopteridaceae, and order Polypodiales. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida, a group of vascular plants that also includes other ferns and their allies. The family Dryopteridaceae is a large group of ferns characterized by their simple, often undivided leaflets and lack of a visible midrib on the underside of their leaves. Nephrodium rigidum is an extinct genus of ferns, meaning it is no longer found in the wild. It is often studied by botanists and paleontologists for its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
uses: Nephrodium rigidum, also known as the Rigid Lady Fern, is an ancient plant with various traditional medicinal uses.
